You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: 2c163c68).

## Issue to Resolve

Change default code style to `ktlint_official`
Starting of ktlint `1.0` the default code style changes from `intellij_idea` (former `kotlin_official`) to `ktlint_official`. The  `ktlint_official` codestyle  adheres to the best parts of  the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android Kotlint styleguid](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). But it no longer is restricted to produce code compatible with the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter which  lacks from bug which still are not resolved after multiple years.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/editorconfig/CodeStyleEditorConfigProperty.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ClassSignatureRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2â†’
 3â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 4â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.EnumValueParser
 5â†’
 6â†’/**
 7â†’ * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
 8â†’ */
 9â†’@Suppress("EnumEntryName")
10â†’public enum class CodeStyleValue {
11â†’    /**
12â†’     * Code formatting based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This
13â†’     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in Android Studio.
14â†’     */
15â†’    android_studio,
16â†’
17â†’    /**
18â†’     * Code formatting based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). This
19â†’     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in IntelliJ IDEA.
20â†’     */
21â†’    intellij_idea,
22â†’
23â†’    /**
24â†’     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions
25â†’     * (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide
26â†’     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
27â†’     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
28â†’     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When
29â†’     * using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) automatic code formatting in the editor. Mean reason
30â†’     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
31â†’     * are still not fixed.
32â†’     * In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
33â†’     */
34â†’    ktlint_official,
35â†’}
36â†’
37â†’public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE: PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType<CodeStyleValue> =
38â†’    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
39â†’        "ktlint_code_style",
40â†’        "The code style ('intellij_idea', 'android_studio' or 'ktlint_official') to be applied. Currently, the " +
41â†’            "value is defaulted to 'intellij_idea' when not set. However, in the future the default code style " +
42â†’            "will be changed to 'ktlint_official'.",
43â†’        SafeEnumValueParser(CodeStyleValue::class.java),
44â†’        CodeStyleValue.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
45â†’    )
46â†’
47â†’public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<CodeStyleValue> =
48â†’    EditorConfigProperty(
49â†’        type = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE,
50â†’        /*
51â†’         * Once the [CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official] is matured, it will become the default code style of ktlint. Until
52â†’         * then the [CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea] is used to remain backwards compatible.
53â†’         */
54â†’        defaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
55â†’        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.android_studio,
56â†’        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
57â†’        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,
58â†’    )
59â†’

1â†’package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2â†’

  5â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS

  8â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COMMA
  9â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST

 21â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED
 22â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible
 23â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 24â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 25â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY
 26â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
 27â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig

 29â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 30â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 31â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY

 33â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 34â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 35â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 36â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 37â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 38â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeSibling
 39â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 40â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 41â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevSibling
 42â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 43â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 44â†’import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 45â†’import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 46â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 47â†’import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafElement
 48â†’
 49â†’/**
 50â†’ * Formats the class signature according to https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#class-headers
 51â†’ *
 52â†’ * In code style `ktlint_official` class headers containing 2 or more parameters are formatted as multiline signature. As the Kotlin Coding
 53â†’ * conventions do not specify what is meant with a "few parameters", no default is set for other code styles.
 54â†’ */
 55â†’public class ClassSignatureRule :
 56â†’    StandardRule(
 57â†’        id = "class-signature",
 58â†’        visitorModifiers =
 59â†’            setOf(
 60â†’                // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 61â†’                RunAsLateAsPossible,
 62â†’                RunAfterRule(DISCOURAGED_COMMENT_LOCATION_RULE_ID, ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED),
 63â†’            ),
 64â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 65â†’            setOf(
 66â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 67â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 68â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 69â†’                FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 70â†’            ),
 71â†’    ),
 72â†’    Rule.Experimental {
 73â†’    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 74â†’    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 75â†’    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 76â†’    private var classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 77â†’
 78â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 79â†’        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 80â†’        classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY]
 81â†’        indentConfig =
 82â†’            IndentConfig(
 83â†’                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 84â†’                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 85â†’            )
 86â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 87â†’    }
 88â†’
 89â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 90â†’        node: ASTNode,
 91â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 92â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 93â†’    ) {
 94â†’        if (node.elementType == CLASS) {
 95â†’            visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 96â†’        }
 97â†’    }
 98â†’
 99â†’    private fun ASTNode.classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean): List<ASTNode> {
100â†’        // Find the nodes that are to be placed on the same line if no max line length is set
101â†’        //     internal class Foo(bar: String) { // Class without super type
102â†’        // or
103â†’        //     public class Foo(bar: String) : Bar { // Class with exactly one super type
104â†’        // or
105â†’        //     private class Foo(bar: String) : // Class with multiple super types which have to be wrapped to next lines
106â†’        val firstCodeChild = getFirstChildInSignature()
107â†’        val lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine =
108â†’            takeIf { excludeSuperTypes }
109â†’                // When the class extends multiple super types or if the super type list contains a newline, all super types are wrapped
110â†’                // on separate line
111â†’                ?.findChildByType(COLON)
112â†’                ?: findChildByType(CLASS_BODY)?.firstChildNode
113â†’        return collectLeavesRecursively()
114â†’            .childrenBetween(
115â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
116â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine },
117â†’            )
118â†’    }
119â†’
120â†’    private fun ASTNode.superTypes() =
121â†’        findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
122â†’            ?.children()
123â†’            ?.filterNot { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() || it.elementType == COMMA }
124â†’
125â†’    private fun ASTNode.hasMultilineSuperTypeList() = findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)?.textContains('\n') == true
126â†’
127â†’    private fun ASTNode.getFirstChildInSignature(): ASTNode? {
128â†’        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
129â†’            ?.let { modifierList ->
130â†’                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
131â†’                var currentNode: ASTNode
132â†’                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
133â†’                    currentNode = iterator.next()
134â†’                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
135â†’                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
136â†’                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
137â†’                    ) {
138â†’                        return currentNode
139â†’                    }
140â†’                }
141â†’                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
142â†’            }
143â†’        return nextCodeLeaf()
144â†’    }
145â†’
146â†’    private fun visitClass(
147â†’        node: ASTNode,
148â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150â†’    ) {
151â†’        require(node.elementType == CLASS)
152â†’
153â†’        val wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters =
154â†’            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
155â†’                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
156â†’                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter()) ||
157â†’                (isMaxLineLengthSet() && classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(node, emit, autoCorrect)) ||
158â†’                (!isMaxLineLengthSet() && node.classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesIsMultiline())
159â†’        fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters, dryRun = false)
160â†’        fixWhitespacesInSuperTypeList(node, emit, autoCorrect, wrappedPrimaryConstructor = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters)
161â†’        fixClassBody(node, emit, autoCorrect)
162â†’    }
163â†’
164â†’    private fun classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(
165â†’        node: ASTNode,
166â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168â†’    ): Boolean {
169â†’        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = true)
170â†’        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, excluding the super types, would be rewritten as single
171â†’        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
172â†’        // actual fix.
173â†’        val length =
174â†’            actualClassSignatureLength +
175â†’                // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
176â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
177â†’        return length > maxLineLength
178â†’    }
179â†’
180â†’    private fun ASTNode.classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesIsMultiline() =
181â†’        classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes = true)
182â†’            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
183â†’
184â†’    private fun ASTNode.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean) =
185â†’        indent(false).length + getClassSignatureNodesLength(excludeSuperTypes)
186â†’
187â†’    private fun ASTNode.getClassSignatureNodesLength(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean) =
188â†’        classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes)
189â†’            .joinTextToString()
190â†’            .length
191â†’
192â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsMultilineParameter(): Boolean =
193â†’        getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
194â†’            ?.children()
195â†’            .orEmpty()
196â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
197â†’            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
198â†’
199â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsAnnotatedParameter(): Boolean =
200â†’        getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
201â†’            ?.children()
202â†’            .orEmpty()
203â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
204â†’            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
205â†’
206â†’    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated() =
207â†’        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
208â†’            ?.children()
209â†’            .orEmpty()
210â†’            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
211â†’
212â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
213â†’        node: ASTNode,
214â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
215â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
216â†’        multiline: Boolean,
217â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
218â†’    ): Int {
219â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
220â†’
221â†’        val primaryConstructorParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
222â†’        val hasNoValueParameters =
223â†’            primaryConstructorParameterList
224â†’                ?.children()
225â†’                .orEmpty()
226â†’                .none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
227â†’
228â†’        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
229â†’            if (hasNoValueParameters) {
230â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
231â†’            } else {
232â†’                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
233â†’                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
234â†’                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
235â†’            }
236â†’
237â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
238â†’    }
239â†’
240â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
241â†’        node: ASTNode,
242â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
243â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
244â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
245â†’    ): Int {
246â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
247â†’
248â†’        node
249â†’            .getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
250â†’            ?.takeUnless { it.containsComment() }
251â†’            ?.let { parameterList ->
252â†’                if (!dryRun) {
253â†’                    emit(parameterList.startOffset, "No parenthesis expected", true)
254â†’                }
255â†’                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
256â†’                    parameterList.treeParent.removeChild(parameterList)
257â†’                } else {
258â†’                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= parameterList.textLength
259â†’                }
260â†’            }
261â†’
262â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
263â†’    }
264â†’
265â†’    private fun ASTNode.containsComment() = children().any { it.isPartOfComment() }
266â†’
267â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
268â†’        node: ASTNode,
269â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
270â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
271â†’        multiline: Boolean,
272â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
273â†’    ): Int {
274â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
275â†’
276â†’        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
277â†’        val firstParameterInList =
278â†’            valueParameterList
279â†’                ?.children()
280â†’                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
281â†’                ?: return 0
282â†’
283â†’        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
284â†’        firstParameter
285â†’            ?.prevLeaf()
286â†’            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
287â†’            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
288â†’                if (multiline) {
289â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
290â†’                        !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
291â†’                    ) {
292â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
293â†’                            emit(firstParameterInList.startOffset, "Newline expected after opening parenthesis", true)
294â†’                        }
295â†’                        // Let indent rule determine the exact indent
296â†’                        val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
297â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
298â†’                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
299â†’                        } else {
300â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
301â†’                        }
302â†’                    }
303â†’                } else {
304â†’                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
305â†’                        if (!dryRun) {
306â†’                            emit(
307â†’                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
308â†’                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
309â†’                                true,
310â†’                            )
311â†’                        }
312â†’                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
313â†’                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
314â†’                        } else {
315â†’                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
316â†’                        }
317â†’                    }
318â†’                }
319â†’            }
320â†’
321â†’        return whiteSpaceCorrection
322â†’    }
323â†’
324â†’    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
325â†’        node: ASTNode,
326â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
327â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
328â†’        multiline: Boolean,
329â†’        dryRun: Boolean,
330â†’    ): Int {
331â†’        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
332â†’
333â†’        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
334â†’        val firstParameterInList =
335â†’            valueParameterList
336â†’                ?.children()
337â†’                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
338â†’                ?: return 0
339â†’
340â†’        valueParameterList
341â†’            .children()
342â†’            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
343â†’            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
344â†’            .forEach { valueParameter ->

567â†’    private fun classSignaturesIncludingFirstSuperTypeExceedsMaxLineLength(
568â†’        node: ASTNode,
569â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
570â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
571â†’    ): Boolean {
572â†’        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = false)
573â†’        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, including the super types, would be rewritten as single
574â†’        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
575â†’        // actual fix.
576â†’        val length =
577â†’            actualClassSignatureLength +
578â†’                // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
579â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
580â†’        return length > maxLineLength
581â†’    }
582â†’

584â†’        findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
585â†’            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
586â†’            ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
587â†’            ?.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() }
588â†’            .isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()

594â†’    ) {

598â†’                if (classBody.prevLeaf()?.text != " ") {
599â†’                    emit(classBody.startOffset, "Expected a single space before class body", true)
600â†’                    if (autoCorrect) {
601â†’                        classBody
602â†’                            .prevLeaf(true)
603â†’                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
604â†’                    }
605â†’                }
606â†’            }
607â†’    }

622â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
623â†’        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
624â†’        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
625â†’    ): List<ASTNode> {
626â†’        val iterator = iterator()
627â†’        var currentNode: ASTNode
628â†’        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
629â†’
630â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
631â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
632â†’            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
633â†’                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
634â†’                break
635â†’            }
636â†’        }
637â†’
638â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
639â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
640â†’            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
641â†’            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
642â†’                break
643â†’            }
644â†’        }
645â†’
646â†’        return childrenBetween
647â†’    }

649â†’    private fun List<ASTNode>.joinTextToString(): String = collectLeavesRecursively().joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }

content.text: Let me look at the beginning of the CHANGELOG and search for related tests:

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1â†’# Changelog
  2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4â†’
  5â†’## Unreleased
  6â†’
  7â†’### Added
  8â†’
  9â†’* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)
 10â†’
 11â†’### Removed
 12â†’
 13â†’### Fixed
 14â†’
 15â†’* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
 16â†’* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
 17â†’* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
 18â†’* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
 19â†’* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
 20â†’* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
 21â†’* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
 22â†’* Do not wrap a binary expression after an elvis operator in case the max line length is exceeded ([#2128](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2128))
 23â†’* Fix indent of IS_EXPRESSION, PREFIX_EXPRESSION and POSTFIX_EXPRESSION in case it contains a linebreak `indent` [#2094](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2094)
 24â†’* Add new experimental rule `function-literal`. This rule enforces the parameter list of a function literal to be formatted consistently. `function-literal` [#2121](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2121)
 25â†’
 26â†’### Changed
 27â†’
 28â†’* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
 29â†’* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
 30â†’* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
 31â†’* As a part of public API stabilization, configure `binary-compatibility-validator` plugin for compile-time verification of binary compatibility with previous `ktlint` versions ([#2131](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2131))
 32â†’
 33â†’## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 34â†’
 35â†’### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 36â†’
 37â†’The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 38â†’
 39â†’API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 40â†’
 41â†’The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 42â†’
 43â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 44â†’
 45â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 46â†’
 47â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 48â†’
 49â†’Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 50â†’
 51â†’* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 52â†’* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 53â†’* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 54â†’
 55â†’Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 56â†’
 57â†’In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 58â†’
 59â†’At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 60â†’
 61â†’At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 62â†’
 63â†’### Added
 64â†’
 65â†’* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 66â†’* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)
 67â†’* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-file` for all code styles. A kotlin (script) file may not be empty ([#1074](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1074))
 68â†’* Add new experimental rule `statement-wrapping` which ensures function, class, or other blocks statement body doesn't start or end at starting or ending braces of the block ([#1938](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1938))
 69â†’* Add new experimental rule `blank-line-before-declaration`. This rule requires a blank line before class, function or property declarations ([#1939](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1939))
 70â†’* Wrap multiple statements on same line `wrapping` ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
 71â†’* Add new rule `ktlint-suppression` to replace the `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives with annotations. This rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` ([#1947](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1947))
 72â†’* Inform user about using `--format` option of KtLint CLI when finding a violation that can be autocorrected ([#1071](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1071))
 73â†’
 74â†’### Removed
 75â†’
 76â†’* Code which was deprecated in `0.49.x` is removed. Consult changelog of 0.49.x` released for more information. Summary of removed code: 
 77â†’
 78â†’### Fixed
 79â†’
 80â†’* Do not flag a (potential) mutable extension property in case the getter is annotated or prefixed with a modifier `property-naming` ([#2024](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2024))
 81â†’* Do not merge an annotated expression body with the function signature even if it fits on a single line ([#2043](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2043))
 82â†’* Ignore property with name `serialVersionUID` in `property-naming` ([#2045](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2045))
 83â†’* Prevent incorrect reporting of violations in case a nullable function type reference exceeds the maximum line length `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1324](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1324)) 
 84â†’* Prevent false negative on `else` branch when body contains only chained calls or binary expression ([#2057](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2057))
 85â†’* Fix indent when property value is wrapped to next line ([#2095](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2095)) 
 86â†’
 87â†’### Changed
 88â†’
 89â†’* Fix Java interoperability issues with `RuleId` and `RuleSetId` classes. Those classes were defined as value classes in `0.49.0` and `0.49.1`. Although the classes were marked with `@JvmInline` it seems that it is not possible to uses those classes from Java base API Consumers like Spotless. The classes have now been replaced with data classes ([#2041](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2041))
 90â†’* Update dependency `info.picocli:picocli` to v4.7.4
 91â†’* Update dependency `org.junit.jupiter:junit-jupiter` to v5.9.3
 92â†’* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.22` and Kotlin version to `1.8.22`.
 93â†’* Update dependency io.github.detekt.sarif4k:sarif4k to v0.4.0
 94â†’* Update dependency org.jetbrains.dokka:dokka-gradle-plugin to v1.8.20
 95â†’* Run format up to 3 times in case formatting introduces changes which also can be autocorrected ([#2084](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2084))
 96â†’
 97â†’## [0.49.1] - 2023-05-12
 98â†’
 99â†’### Added
100â†’
101â†’### Removed
102â†’
103â†’### Fixed
104â†’* Store path of file containing a lint violation relative to the location of the baseline file itself ([#1962](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1962))
105â†’* Print absolute path of file in lint violations when flag "--relative" is not specified in Ktlint CLI ([#1963](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1963)) 
106â†’* Handle parameter `--code-style=android_studio` in Ktlint CLI identical to deprecated parameter `--android` ([#1982](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1982))
107â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if class without body is followed by multiple blank lines until end of file `no-consecutive-blank-lines` ([#1987](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1987))
108â†’* Allow to 'unset' the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` when using `ktlint_official` code style `function-signature` ([#1977](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1977))
109â†’* Prevent nullpointer exception (NPE) if or operator at start of line is followed by dot qualified expression `indent` ([#1993](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1993))
110â†’* Fix indentation of multiline parameter list in function literal `indent` ([#1976](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1976))
111â†’* Restrict indentation of closing quotes to `ktlint_official` code style to keep formatting of other code styles consistent with `0.48.x` and before `indent` ([#1971](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1971))
112â†’* Extract rule `no-single-line-block-comment` from `comment-wrapping` rule. The `no-single-line-block-comment` rule is added as experimental rule to the `ktlint_official` code style, but it can be enabled explicitly for the other code styles as well. ([#1980](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1980))
113â†’* Clean-up unwanted logging dependencies ([#1998](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1998))
114â†’* Fix directory traversal for patterns referring to paths outside of current working directory or any of it child directories ([#2002](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2002))
115â†’* Prevent multiple expressions on same line separated by semicolon ([#1078](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1078))
116â†’
117â†’### Changed
118â†’
119â†’* Moved class `Baseline` from `ktlint-cli` to `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline` so that Baseline functionality is reusable for API Consumers.
120â†’
121â†’## [0.49.0] - 2023-04-21
122â†’
123â†’WARNING: This version of KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgraded before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
124â†’
125â†’All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
126â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
127â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
128â†’* The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
129â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
130â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
131â†’
132â†’### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
133â†’
134â†’The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
135â†’
136â†’Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
137â†’* The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI.
138â†’* The `.editorconfig` properties used to enable or disable rule and rule sets. Note that properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` have been removed in this release. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
139â†’* The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
140â†’* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
141â†’
142â†’### Promote experimental rules to non-experimental
143â†’
144â†’The rules below have been promoted to non-experimental rules:
145â†’* [block-comment-initial-star-alignment](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#block-comment-initial-star-alignment)
146â†’* [class-naming](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#classobject-naming)
147â†’* [comment-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#comment-wrapping)
148â†’* [function-return-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-return-type-spacing)
149â†’* [function-start-of-body-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-start-of-body-spacing)
150â†’* [function-type-reference-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#function-type-reference-spacing)
151â†’* [fun-keyword-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#fun-keyword-spacing)
152â†’* [kdoc-wrapping](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#kdoc-wrapping)
153â†’* [modifier-list-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#modifier-list-spacing)
154â†’* [nullable-type-spacing](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#nullable-type-spacing)
155â†’* [spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#spacing-between-function-name-and-opening-parenthesis)
156â†’* [unnecessary-parentheses-before-trailing-lambda](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/standard/#unnecessary-parenthesis-before-trailing-lambda)
157â†’
158â†’Note that this only affects users that have enabled the standard ruleset while having the experimental rules disabled.
159â†’
160â†’### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
161â†’
162â†’This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
163â†’
164â†’This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
165â†’
166â†’#### Experimental rules
167â†’
168â†’Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
169â†’
170â†’When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
171â†’
172â†’#### EditorConfig
173â†’
174â†’Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
175â†’
176â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
177â†’
178â†’#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
179â†’
180â†’A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
181â†’
182â†’This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
183â†’
184â†’The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
185â†’
186â†’The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
187â†’
188â†’Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
189â†’
190â†’#### Package restructuring and class relocation
191â†’
192â†’The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
193â†’
194â†’This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
195â†’
196â†’Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
197â†’
198â†’| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
199â†’|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
200â†’| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |

{"file_path": "/project/ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/editorconfig/CodeStyleEditorConfigProperty.kt", "content": "package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig

import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.EnumValueParser

/**
 * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
 */
@Suppress("EnumEntryName")
public enum class CodeStyleValue {
    /**
     * Code formatting based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This
     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in Android Studio.
     */
    android_studio,

    /**
     * Code formatting based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). This
     * code style aims to be compatible with code formatting in IntelliJ IDEA.
     */
    intellij_idea,

    /**
     * Code formatting based on the best of both the Kotlin Coding conventions
     * (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide
     * (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics
     * which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats
     * code in a way which is not compatible with the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. When
     * using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) automatic code formatting in the editor. Mean reason
     * for deviating from the code formatting provided by the editor is that those contain bugs which after some years
     * are still not fixed.
     * In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
     */
    ktlint_official,
}

public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE: PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType<CodeStyleValue> =
    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
        "ktlint_code_style",
        "The code style ('intellij_idea', 'android_studio' or 'ktlint_official') to be applied. " +
            "The default code style is 'ktlint_official'.",
        SafeEnumValueParser(CodeStyleValue::class.java),
        CodeStyleValue.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
    )

public val CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<CodeStyleValue> =
    EditorConfigProperty(
        type = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY_TYPE,
        defaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,
        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.android_studio,
        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea,
        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official,
    )
"}

240â†’    fun `Given an editorconfig containing a property with undefined type then retrieving that property via the EditConfig may not result in an exception`() {
241â†’        // The Property and PropertyValue builders of the ec4j library do not allow to build a property having field "Property.type" set to
242â†’        // null. However, when loading an ".editorconfig" file containing a property with a name for which no property type exists with that
243â†’        // same name, this does result in such a property. It takes a detour to create the property:
244â†’        //  - Create an ".editorconfig" file on the file system mock
245â†’        //  - Load the ".editorconfig" via the EditorConfigDefaults loader
246â†’        //  - Find the property which is set in the ".editorconfig" file and validate that its type is null
247â†’        //  - Transform the property to a KtLint EditorConfig
248â†’        //  - Extract the property of the KtLint EditorConfig
249â†’        // Note that two properties are created which are identical except that only one of the properties has a null type and the other
250â†’        // property has a correctly defined type.
251â†’        //language=
252â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = "ktlint_test_rule-1"
253â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1)
254â†’        //language=
255â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = "ktlint_test_rule-2"
256â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2)
257â†’
258â†’        val ktlintTestFileSystem =
259â†’            KtlintTestFileSystem().apply {
260â†’                writeRootEditorConfigFile(
261â†’                    //language=EditorConfig
262â†’                    """
263â†’                    [*.{kt,kts}]
264â†’                    $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = disabled
265â†’                    $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = disabled
266â†’                    """.trimIndent(),
267â†’                )
268â†’            }
269â†’
270â†’        val ktlintTestRuleProperties =
271â†’            EditorConfigDefaults
272â†’                .load(
273â†’                    path = ktlintTestFileSystem.resolve(""),
274â†’                    propertyTypes =
275â†’                        setOf(
276â†’                            // Note that ktlintTestRuleEditorConfigPropertyType1 has been left out on purpose
277â†’                            ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2.type,
278â†’                        ),
279â†’                ).value
280â†’                .sections
281â†’                .flatMap { it.properties.values }
282â†’                .associateBy { it.name }
283â†’
284â†’        // Validate that both properties are loaded
285â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1] != null) {
286â†’            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
287â†’        }
288â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2] != null) {
289â†’            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
290â†’        }
291â†’
292â†’        // Validate that the type of one of the properties is null
293â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1]?.type == null) {
294â†’            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' should have an undefined type"
295â†’        }
296â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2]?.type != null) {
297â†’            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' should have a defined type"
298â†’        }
299â†’
300â†’        val editorConfig = EditorConfig(ktlintTestRuleProperties)
301â†’
302â†’        // Although the type of one of the properties is null, both can be loaded as the type of EditorConfigProperty is used to parse the
303â†’        // raw value of the property
304â†’        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
305â†’        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
306â†’    }
307â†’
308â†’    private fun editorConfigProperty(name: String) =
309â†’        EditorConfigProperty(
310â†’            name = name,
311â†’            type =
312â†’                PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
313â†’                    name,
314â†’                    "",
315â†’                    SafeEnumValueParser(RuleExecution::class.java),
316â†’                    RuleExecution.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
317â†’                ),
318â†’            defaultValue = RuleExecution.enabled,
319â†’        )
320â†’
321â†’    private companion object {
322â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_NAME = "some-property-name"
323â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE = "some-property-value"
324â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO = "some-property-value-android"
325â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT = "some-property-value-default"
326â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA = "some-property-value-intellij-idea"
327â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL = "some-property-value-official"
328â†’        val SOME_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY =
329â†’            EditorConfigProperty(
330â†’                name = SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
331â†’                type =
332â†’                    PropertyType(
333â†’                        SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
334â†’                        "",
335â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.IDENTITY_VALUE_PARSER,
336â†’                        setOf(SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO, SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA),
337â†’                    ),
338â†’                defaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT,
339â†’                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO,
340â†’                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL,
341â†’                intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA,
342â†’            )
343â†’    }
344â†’}
345â†’

content.text:
I see that there are tests that need to be updated. Let me look at the specific test in `EditorConfigTest.kt` that checks for the default code style:

Looking at line 39-40 in `EditorConfigTest.kt`:
```kotlin
// Note that IntelliJ IDEA is the default code style for now, this will change to KTLINT_OFFICIAL in the future
assertThat(actual).isEqualTo(SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA)
```

This test needs to be updated because the default code style is now `ktlint_official`.

Also, looking at line 223:
```kotlin
assertThat(actual).isEqualTo(SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA)
```

This test is for when a property value is "unset" - it should now return the ktlint_official default value.

Let me update these tests.

240â†’        // The Property and PropertyValue builders of the ec4j library do not allow to build a property having field "Property.type" set to
241â†’        // null. However, when loading an ".editorconfig" file containing a property with a name for which no property type exists with that
242â†’        // same name, this does result in such a property. It takes a detour to create the property:
243â†’        //  - Create an ".editorconfig" file on the file system mock
244â†’        //  - Load the ".editorconfig" via the EditorConfigDefaults loader
245â†’        //  - Find the property which is set in the ".editorconfig" file and validate that its type is null
246â†’        //  - Transform the property to a KtLint EditorConfig
247â†’        //  - Extract the property of the KtLint EditorConfig
248â†’        // Note that two properties are created which are identical except that only one of the properties has a null type and the other
249â†’        // property has a correctly defined type.
250â†’        //language=
251â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = "ktlint_test_rule-1"
252â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1)
253â†’        //language=
254â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = "ktlint_test_rule-2"
255â†’        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2)
256â†’
257â†’        val ktlintTestFileSystem =
258â†’            KtlintTestFileSystem().apply {
259â†’                writeRootEditorConfigFile(
260â†’                    //language=EditorConfig
261â†’                    """
262â†’                    [*.{kt,kts}]
263â†’                    $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = disabled
264â†’                    $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = disabled
265â†’                    """.trimIndent(),
266â†’                )
267â†’            }
268â†’
269â†’        val ktlintTestRuleProperties =
270â†’            EditorConfigDefaults
271â†’                .load(
272â†’                    path = ktlintTestFileSystem.resolve(""),
273â†’                    propertyTypes =
274â†’                        setOf(
275â†’                            // Note that ktlintTestRuleEditorConfigPropertyType1 has been left out on purpose
276â†’                            ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2.type,
277â†’                        ),
278â†’                ).value
279â†’                .sections
280â†’                .flatMap { it.properties.values }
281â†’                .associateBy { it.name }
282â†’
283â†’        // Validate that both properties are loaded
284â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1] != null) {
285â†’            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
286â†’        }
287â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2] != null) {
288â†’            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
289â†’        }
290â†’
291â†’        // Validate that the type of one of the properties is null
292â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1]?.type == null) {
293â†’            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' should have an undefined type"
294â†’        }
295â†’        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2]?.type != null) {
296â†’            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' should have a defined type"
297â†’        }
298â†’
299â†’        val editorConfig = EditorConfig(ktlintTestRuleProperties)
300â†’
301â†’        // Although the type of one of the properties is null, both can be loaded as the type of EditorConfigProperty is used to parse the
302â†’        // raw value of the property
303â†’        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
304â†’        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
305â†’    }
306â†’
307â†’    private fun editorConfigProperty(name: String) =
308â†’        EditorConfigProperty(
309â†’            name = name,
310â†’            type =
311â†’                PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
312â†’                    name,
313â†’                    "",
314â†’                    SafeEnumValueParser(RuleExecution::class.java),
315â†’                    RuleExecution.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
316â†’                ),
317â†’            defaultValue = RuleExecution.enabled,
318â†’        )
319â†’
320â†’    private companion object {
321â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_NAME = "some-property-name"
322â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE = "some-property-value"
323â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO = "some-property-value-android"
324â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT = "some-property-value-default"
325â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA = "some-property-value-intellij-idea"
326â†’        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL = "some-property-value-official"
327â†’        val SOME_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY =
328â†’            EditorConfigProperty(
329â†’                name = SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
330â†’                type =
331â†’                    PropertyType(
332â†’                        SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
333â†’                        "",
334â†’                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.IDENTITY_VALUE_PARSER,
335â†’                        setOf(SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO, SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA),
336â†’                    ),
337â†’                defaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT,
338â†’                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO,
339â†’                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL,
340â†’                intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA,
341â†’            )

 55â†’public class ClassSignatureRule :

 78â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {

 99â†’    private fun ASTNode.classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean): List<ASTNode> {
100â†’        // Find the nodes that are to be placed on the same line if no max line length is set
101â†’        //     internal class Foo(bar: String) { // Class without super type
102â†’        // or
103â†’        //     public class Foo(bar: String) : Bar { // Class with exactly one super type
104â†’        // or
105â†’        //     private class Foo(bar: String) : // Class with multiple super types which have to be wrapped to next lines
106â†’        val firstCodeChild = getFirstChildInSignature()
107â†’        val lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine =
108â†’            takeIf { excludeSuperTypes }
109â†’                // When the class extends multiple super types or if the super type list contains a newline, all super types are wrapped
110â†’                // on separate line
111â†’                ?.findChildByType(COLON)
112â†’                ?: findChildByType(CLASS_BODY)?.firstChildNode
113â†’        return collectLeavesRecursively()
114â†’            .childrenBetween(
115â†’                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
116â†’                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine },
117â†’            )
118â†’    }

128â†’        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
129â†’            ?.let { modifierList ->
130â†’                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
131â†’                var currentNode: ASTNode
132â†’                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
133â†’                    currentNode = iterator.next()
134â†’                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
135â†’                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
136â†’                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
137â†’                    ) {
138â†’                        return currentNode
139â†’                    }
140â†’                }
141â†’                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
142â†’            }

146â†’    private fun visitClass(
147â†’        node: ASTNode,
148â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150â†’    ) {
151â†’        require(node.elementType == CLASS)
152â†’
153â†’        val wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters =
154â†’            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
155â†’                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
156â†’                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter()) ||
157â†’                (isMaxLineLengthSet() && classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(node, emit, autoCorrect)) ||
158â†’                (!isMaxLineLengthSet() && node.classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesIsMultiline())
159â†’        fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters, dryRun = false)
160â†’        fixWhitespacesInSuperTypeList(node, emit, autoCorrect, wrappedPrimaryConstructor = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters)
161â†’        fixClassBody(node, emit, autoCorrect)

164â†’    private fun classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(
165â†’        node: ASTNode,
166â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168â†’    ): Boolean {
169â†’        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = true)
170â†’        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, excluding the super types, would be rewritten as single
171â†’        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
172â†’        // actual fix.
173â†’        val length =
174â†’            actualClassSignatureLength +
175â†’                // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
176â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)

213â†’        node: ASTNode,

567â†’    private fun classSignaturesIncludingFirstSuperTypeExceedsMaxLineLength(
568â†’        node: ASTNode,
569â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
570â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
571â†’    ): Boolean {
572â†’        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = false)
573â†’        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, including the super types, would be rewritten as single
574â†’        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
575â†’        // actual fix.
576â†’        val length =
577â†’            actualClassSignatureLength +
578â†’                // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
579â†’                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
580â†’        return length > maxLineLength
581â†’    }

623â†’        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
624â†’        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
625â†’    ): List<ASTNode> {
626â†’        val iterator = iterator()
627â†’        var currentNode: ASTNode
628â†’        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
629â†’
630â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
631â†’            currentNode = iterator.next()
632â†’            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
633â†’                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
634â†’                break
635â†’            }
636â†’        }
637â†’
638â†’        while (iterator.hasNext()) {

641â†’            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
